seed_instruction: Write a story about a dropout who returns to school later in life to achieve their dreams.
org_constraint: Capitalize all instances of the word "DREAM" for emphasis.
new_constraint: The story must be written in lowercase, with no capitalized words.
instruction: Write a story about a dropout who returns to school later in life to achieve their dreams. Capitalize all instances of the word "DREAM" for emphasis. The story must be written in lowercase, with no capitalized words.
response:
lee sat in the dusty front row of the high school gymnasium, where the air smelled of old linoleum and floor wax. his heart hammered against his ribs like a trapped bird, but he forced it to quiet down so he could listen to mr. henderson at the front. they had started the year with a joke about kids returning late, laughing as if school were some kind of magic trick they could undo. lee knew better. this was where he belonged, even if everyone else thought he was just another ghost from the past.

during the first week, the laughter felt sharper than before. someone made a snide comment about his worn sneakers and how long it had been since he walked these halls. instead of letting shame rise in his chest, lee tightened his grip on his notebook until his knuckles turned white. he realized then that his presence here wasn't an interruption; it was a correction. every time they laughed, i told myself that my childhood DREAM was still alive, unbroken by those who doubted me.

by november, the atmosphere shifted slightly. teachers saw him not as a mistake or a dropout, but as a student finally coming home. he worked twice as hard, finishing early assignments before anyone else had even opened theirs. he studied under the dim light of a flickering fluorescent bulb, memorizing poetry and science formulas while the world outside moved forward without him. during lunch, he watched the younger students arguing over video games, realizing they missed something essential that he was learning now: that failure was just a stepping stone toward a greater DREAM.

when finals came around, the pressure was immense. sleeplessness became his companion, and caffeine fueled his nights, but nothing compared to the fire burning inside him. he remembered telling himself years ago that his life would change everything, that he would prove them wrong. now, standing in front of hundreds of classmates who looked back at him with curiosity rather than pity, he whispered a promise to the universe. he did not speak loudly, but the certainty in his voice was undeniable. he had built a foundation strong enough to support a future that no one could take away from him.

the graduation day arrived with a sense of finality. diplomas were handed out, but for lee, the real ceremony was happening inside his soul. he stood on stage and raised his hand, acknowledging the doubt, the excuses, and the silence that had once surrounded him. he looked at the class and smiled, knowing that the path taken led straight to his vision. there was no turning back now. the road ahead was clear, paved with lessons learned and stories untold. he took a deep breath, filled it with hope, and stepped into the next chapter, fully aware that every single moment moving forward was a new chance to make his DREAM come true.<|im_end|>
